scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope work stayed specific to registered-function obligation mapping and produced a usable NERC control matrix tied to retained evidence and owners.
Missing evidence blocked readiness and forced an explicit gap summary rather than an unsupported audit-prepared conclusion.
Conflicting source interpretation was escalated instead of collapsed into unsupported certainty.
Legal interpretation and regulator-negotiation work were refused cleanly and routed to the proper adjacent owners.
The pack rejected a specialized CIP request instead of pretending generic NERC compliance handling was enough.
Operational practice conflicting with record-retention expectations was treated as a control failure requiring remediation.
Missing registered-function and jurisdiction context produced a blocked low-confidence disposition instead of fabricated applicability logic.
Self-log and remediation support stayed bounded to evidence packaging and owner routing without implying enforcement decisions.
Audit and attestation preparation used NERC-specific terminology, Align or RSAW artifact shapes, and named evidence dependencies.
Safety-critical operating authority was routed out of lane while preserving compliance findings for downstream owners.
Registration and applicability changes were handled as supportable prerequisites and escalation paths, not as final registry decisions.
Tenant-specific adaptation preserved the public NERC and FERC baseline and recorded assumptions instead of silently mutating authority boundaries.

notes: Scenario suite demonstrates NERC-specific registration, CMEP, internal-controls, Align and SEL, attestation, and exception-handling behavior rather than generic compliance support.
All required abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus cross-industry are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows.
